isolation.command: cd agent && npx vitest run src/__tests__/sanitize.test.ts src/__tests__/time-parser.test.ts --reporter=verbose

steps.action: Read sanitize.test.ts to identify tests for trailing junk stripping
steps.expected: Tests exist that verify trailing junk characters are removed
steps.observed: 6 tests cover trailing junk: unmatched brackets (']'), multiple trailing junk (']]}''), pipe/tilde ('|~'), backslash/backtick ('\`'), combined whitespace+junk, and a realistic noisy input

steps.action: Read sanitize.ts source to verify implementation
steps.expected: Implementation strips trailing junk characters
steps.observed: TRAILING_JUNK_RE = /[\]\[}{|\\~`]+$/ regex strips trailing junk, applied after whitespace normalization

steps.action: Run vitest with verbose output
steps.expected: All trailing junk tests pass
steps.observed: All 6 trailing junk tests pass (0ms each)

relevantTests: should strip trailing unmatched brackets
should strip multiple trailing junk characters
should strip trailing pipe and tilde characters
should strip trailing backslash and backtick characters
should handle combined whitespace and trailing junk
should handle a realistic noisy input

steps.action: Read sanitize.test.ts to identify whitespace normalization tests
steps.expected: Tests for trimming, collapsing multiple spaces, and normalizing tabs
steps.observed: 4 tests cover whitespace: trim leading/trailing, collapse multiple spaces, normalize tabs/mixed whitespace, and empty-string for whitespace-only input

steps.action: Read sanitize.ts source to verify implementation
steps.expected: MULTI_SPACE_RE collapses all whitespace runs to single space
steps.observed: MULTI_SPACE_RE = /\s+/g replaces all whitespace runs (spaces, tabs, newlines) with single space, preceded by trim()

steps.action: Run vitest with verbose output
steps.expected: All whitespace tests pass
steps.observed: All 4 whitespace normalization tests pass (0-1ms each)

relevantTests: should trim leading and trailing whitespace
should collapse multiple consecutive spaces into one
should normalize tabs and mixed whitespace to single spaces
should return empty string for whitespace-only input

assertions.title: Time parsed in user timezone by default

steps.action: Read time-parser.test.ts to identify timezone default tests
steps.expected: Test verifies time is parsed in user's configured timezone
steps.observed: Test 'timezone default to user TZ (VAL-PARSE-003)' parses 'at 8:50' with tz='America/Los_Angeles', reference 2PM PST, and asserts hour=20 and minute=50 in LA timezone

steps.action: Read time-parser.ts source to verify implementation
steps.expected: parseTime uses user timezone for chrono-node reference
steps.observed: buildReference() passes userTimezone to chrono-node as the timezone context. createChronoInstance() uses userTimezone for AM/PM resolution. No UTC assumption.

steps.action: Run vitest with verbose output
steps.expected: Timezone default test passes
steps.observed: Test passes (10ms)

relevantTests: should parse "at 8:50" in user timezone (America/Los_Angeles)

steps.action: Read time-parser.test.ts to identify explicit timezone tests
steps.expected: Test verifies explicit timezone token overrides user default
steps.observed: Test 'explicit timezone override (VAL-PARSE-004)' parses 'at 8:50 EST' with user tz='America/Los_Angeles', verifies hour=20 and minute=50 at EST offset (UTC-5/-300min)

steps.action: Read time-parser.ts source to verify implementation
steps.expected: Explicit timezone abbreviations are detected and passed to chrono-node
steps.observed: EXPLICIT_TZ_RE detects timezone abbreviations, TIMEZONE_OFFSETS maps them to minute offsets, passed to chrono-node via timezones option

steps.action: Run vitest with verbose output
steps.expected: Explicit timezone test passes
steps.observed: Test passes (18ms)

relevantTests: should parse "at 8:50 EST" using EST instead of user timezone

steps.action: Read time-parser.test.ts to identify no-day-defaults-to-today tests
steps.expected: Test verifies that time without day reference defaults to today
steps.observed: Test 'no-day defaults to today (VAL-PARSE-005)' parses 'at 9pm' with ref 2PM March 15, asserts parsedDay === refDay (same day)

steps.action: Read time-parser.ts source to verify implementation
steps.expected: chrono-node defaults to today when no day specified
steps.observed: adjustPastTimeToTomorrow only advances date when parsedDate < referenceDate AND no certain day. For future times on same day, date stays as today.

steps.action: Run vitest with verbose output
steps.expected: No-day test passes
steps.observed: Test passes (0ms)

relevantTests: should default to today when no day is mentioned and time is in the future

assertions.title: Task text clean of time residue

steps.action: Read time-parser.test.ts to identify task text cleanup tests
steps.expected: Tests verify time fragments removed from stored task text
steps.observed: 3 tests in 'task text cleanup — no time residue (VAL-PARSE-006)': (1) 'remind me to get food at 8:50' → taskText contains 'remind me to get food' without '8:50' or 'at', (2) time-only input → empty taskText, (3) 'call mom at 3pm' → 'call mom' with no dangling 'at'

steps.action: Read time-parser.ts extractTaskText function
steps.expected: Implementation removes matched time fragments and cleans prepositions
steps.observed: extractTaskText removes chrono results from text by index, then cleans trailing prepositions (at/on/by/from/until/before/after/around) and collapses whitespace

steps.action: Run vitest with verbose output
steps.expected: All task text cleanup tests pass
steps.observed: All 3 tests pass (0ms each)

relevantTests: should remove time fragment from task text
should return empty taskText when input is only a time expression
should clean up dangling prepositions after time removal

steps.action: Read time-parser.test.ts to identify chrono-before-LLM test
steps.expected: Test verifies chrono-node runs without LLM involvement
steps.observed: Test 'chrono-node called before LLM (VAL-PARSE-007)' creates a mockLlmCall spy, calls parseTime, asserts mockLlmCall was not called

steps.action: Read time-parser.ts source imports and function signature
steps.expected: parseTime doesn't import or accept any LLM client
steps.observed: time-parser.ts imports only 'chrono-node' and local types. parseTime(text, userTimezone, referenceDate) has no LLM parameter. The module is structurally incapable of calling an LLM.

steps.action: Run vitest with verbose output
steps.expected: Chrono-before-LLM test passes
steps.observed: Test passes (0ms)

relevantTests: should use chrono-node for parsing without any LLM call

steps.action: Read time-parser.test.ts to identify past-time-to-tomorrow tests
steps.expected: Test verifies past time is advanced to next day
steps.observed: 2 tests in 'past time defaults to tomorrow (VAL-EDGE-001)': (1) 'at 8am' at 10am reference → parsedDay = refDay+1 (tomorrow), (2) 'tomorrow at 8am' with explicit day → stays at refDay+1 (doesn't double-advance)

steps.action: Read time-parser.ts adjustPastTimeToTomorrow
steps.expected: Past times without explicit day are advanced by 24h
steps.observed: adjustPastTimeToTomorrow checks: if no certain day AND parsedDate < referenceDate, adds 24*60*60*1000 ms. Explicit day/weekday skips the adjustment.

steps.action: Run vitest with verbose output
steps.expected: Both past-time tests pass
steps.observed: Both tests pass (0-1ms)

relevantTests: should default to tomorrow when time is already past for today
should NOT advance to tomorrow when an explicit day is specified

steps.action: Read time-parser.test.ts to identify AM/PM ambiguity tests
steps.expected: Tests verify contextual AM/PM resolution based on time of day
steps.observed: 4 tests in 'AM/PM contextual resolution (VAL-EDGE-005)': (1) bare '8' in afternoon → 8PM (hour=20), (2) bare '8' in morning → 8AM (hour=8), (3) explicit 'am' overrides afternoon context → 8AM, (4) explicit 'pm' overrides morning context → 8PM

steps.action: Read time-parser.ts createChronoInstance AM/PM refiner
steps.expected: Custom chrono refiner applies contextual AM/PM defaults
steps.observed: Custom refiner checks if meridiem is uncertain: if currentHour>=12, assigns PM (meridiem=1, hour+12); if currentHour<12, assigns AM (meridiem=0). Only applies to hours 1-12.

steps.action: Run vitest with verbose output
steps.expected: All AM/PM ambiguity tests pass
steps.observed: All 4 tests pass (0ms each)

relevantTests: should default bare "8" to PM when current time is afternoon
should default bare "8" to AM when current time is morning
should respect explicit AM even in afternoon context
should respect explicit PM even in morning context

summary: Tested 9 assertions: 9 passed, 0 failed, 0 blocked. All parsing and edge-case assertions are covered by meaningful, passing unit tests in sanitize.test.ts (21 tests) and time-parser.test.ts (19 tests). Total: 40 tests, all green.
